# API & Integration Checklist: Manifold Markets API Plugin & MCP Server

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of the API & integration requirements in `spec.md` — a "unit test for the English" of the spec, not a test of the implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-20
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. Items evaluate the REQUIREMENTS THEMSELVES (completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, coverage) — not whether the implementation works.

## Requirement Completeness — Endpoint Surface

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements present for every public REST endpoint category (markets, users, groups, bets, comments, transactions, leagues, boosts, authenticated writes), or is any category left unspecified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014…§FR-019] — PASS: FR-014 through FR-019 cover all 8 categories; FR-013 enumerates 15 write actions.
- [x] CHK002 Is the full enumeration of authenticated write actions (bet, multi-bet, cancel, create market, resolve, sell, close, add liquidity, add answer, tag/untag, rebalance, add/award bounty, send mana, comment) explicitly required, or does the spec leave any write action implicit? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013] — PASS: FR-013 lists all 15 explicitly.
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements defined for the batch-probabilities endpoint (up to 100 IDs) including the documented maximum, or is the batch limit left unspecified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014, Gap] — PASS: FR-014 states "up to 100 market IDs"; edge case requires documenting the limit in the tool description.
- [x] CHK004 Is a requirement present that bounds the integration to `visibility: 'public'` endpoints only, with an explicit prohibition on undocumented/private endpoints? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004] — PASS: FR-004 explicitly prohibits undocumented/private endpoints.
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements specified for the pagination cursor on the list-markets endpoint ("before"), including ordering and no-gap/no-duplicate behavior across pages? [Completeness, Spec §US1 scenario 6, FR-014] — PASS: US1 scenario 6 specifies "before" cursor, stable chronological order, no gaps or duplicates.

## Requirement Clarity — Parameter & Contract Semantics

- [x] CHK006 Is "equivalent parameter schemas" between the plugin and MCP server (SC-005) defined with a concrete equivalence criterion, or is equivalence left ambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §SC-005, Ambiguity] — PASS: SC-005 defines behavioral equivalence ("tool call that succeeds against one succeeds against the other with the same response shape"); research D1 confirms structural identity by construction via shared definitions.
- [x] CHK007 Is the boundary of "human-readable formatting" (probability-to-percentage) precisely scoped so it cannot be read as license to mutate other response fields? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003] — PASS (resolved Q4): formatting exception clause removed; FR-003 now mandates verbatim passthrough with zero transformation. Question is moot.
- [x] CHK008 Are the bet-filter parameters (user, market, answer, time bounds, kind, amount, redemption filtering) enumerated with enough specificity to derive a parameter schema without guessing? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016] — PASS: FR-016 enumerates categories; contracts/tools.md maps to exact params (userId, contractId, contractSlug, answerId, before/after/beforeTime/afterTime, kinds, minAmount, filterRedemptions).
- [x] CHK009 Is "thin wrapper, zero business logic" (FR-003) defined with an explicit list of what counts as forbidden logic (aggregation, derived state, caching), or could it be interpreted permissively? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Ambiguity] — PASS: FR-003 forbids aggregate, cache (beyond HTTP), mutate/transform; Q4 "zero transformation" closes the loophole.
- [x] CHK010 Is the requirement that each tool wraps exactly ONE endpoint stated unambiguously, including for grouped endpoints like `/v0/user/[username]` vs `/v0/user/[username]/lite`? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003] — PASS: FR-003 "exactly one endpoint"; contracts confirm /lite variants are separate tools.

## Requirement Consistency — Auth Gating & Platform Parity

- [x] CHK011 Do the auth-gating requirements align across FR-006 (read-only needs no key; authenticated tools require key), US3 scenario 11, and the edge case for missing keys — i.e., is the "API key required" error contract stated consistently everywhere? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, §US3-11, §Edge Cases] — PASS (resolved Q3): all four spots (FR-006, US3-11, edge cases 108/112) reconciled to rely-on-upstream; no contradictions remain.
- [x] CHK012 Is the API-key channel requirement (FR-007: HTTPS, `Authorization: Key` header, never logged) consistent with the security assumptions and the credential-leak edge case? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, §Assumptions, §Edge Cases] — PASS: FR-007 states MUST HTTPS + MUST NEVER logged; assumptions align.
- [x] CHK013 Is the shared-client / platform-parity requirement (FR-009) consistent with SC-005 (equivalent tool inventory and response shape across plugin and MCP server)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, §SC-005] — PASS: FR-009 shared client sole HTTP owner → same behavior → SC-005 holds by construction.
- [x] CHK014 Is the tool-naming-prefix requirement (`manifold_`, FR-005) applied consistently to both the plugin tool list and the MCP server's `tools/list` expectation (US4 scenario 1)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, §US4-1] — PASS: FR-005 mandates prefix; US4 scenario 1 expects same `manifold_`-prefixed tools.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality — Measurability

- [x] CHK015 Can "every one of the ~30 public endpoints exposed, no missing, no private" (SC-002) be objectively verified without an implementation — i.e., is the canonical endpoint list referenced or bounded? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002] — PASS: verifiable by comparing tool inventory against Manifold schema's visibility:'public' endpoints; "~30" is approximate (actual 40 per contracts).
- [x] CHK016 Can "manifest-declared tools exactly match exported tools" (SC-004/FR-010) be verified by a static check, and is the verification step named as a build/validate gate? [Measurability, Spec §FR-010, §SC-004] — PASS: FR-010 "statically verifiable by the plugin build/validate step"; SC-004 "verified automatically on every build."
- [x] CHK017 Is "clear error" (FR-006, FR-012) for missing-key and upstream failures defined with measurable properties (includes status + body, no silent empty success), or is "clear" left subjective? [Measurability, Spec §FR-006, §FR-012, §SC-008, Ambiguity] — PASS (resolved Q1): FR-021 defines uniform error shape {category, message, status?, body?}; SC-008 references it; no longer subjective.
- [x] CHK018 Can "the integration's automated tests pass against the live API" (SC-010) be assessed from the requirements alone, given test separation between public and auth-required suites is specified? [Measurability, Spec §SC-010, §FR-020] — PASS (resolved Q2): FR-020/SC-010 specify "all ~16 public read-only endpoints (full coverage, no sampling)"; auth tests clearly separated.

## Scenario Coverage — API Flows

- [x] CHK019 Are requirements defined for the primary read flow (search → fetch by ID/slug → read probability/positions) as a single end-to-end journey, not just isolated endpoints? [Coverage, Spec §US1, §SC-001] — PASS: US1 has 6 acceptance scenarios covering the full journey; SC-001 validates end-to-end.
- [x] CHK020 Are requirements specified for the full authenticated trading journey (lookup → bet → confirm in history → sell) as an integrated flow? [Coverage, Spec §US3, §SC-006] — PASS: US3 scenarios 3/4/7 + SC-006 cover the full journey.
- [x] CHK021 Are alternate scenarios defined for the multi-choice/multi-bet path (multi-bet across answers, add-answer to multi-choice) distinct from single-bet requirements? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013, Gap] — PASS (fixed 2026-06-20): US3 scenario 12 added exercising multi-bet across multiple answers; SC-006 extended to cover multi-choice markets via the multi-bet tool.
- [x] CHK022 Are requirements specified for the standalone-MCP-server flow (list tools → call read tool → call write tool) as scenarios, not only as a parity statement? [Coverage, Spec §US4, §SC-005] — PASS: US4 has 4 acceptance scenarios (list, read, write, missing-key error); scenario-based, not just parity.

## Edge Case Coverage — API Boundary Conditions

- [x] CHK023 Are requirements defined for upstream non-2xx responses (404 unknown ID, 401 bad key, 429 rate limit) including that status AND body are surfaced? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-012, §SC-008] — PASS: edge case line 104 + FR-012 + FR-021 all require status+body in the uniform error shape.
- [x] CHK024 Is the empty/malformed required path-parameter case required to be rejected client-side before the HTTP call? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-008] — PASS: edge case line 105 "MUST reject it before making the request with a parameter-validation error."
- [x] CHK025 Is the omitted-optional-parameter case required to be excluded from the upstream request (vs. sent as null/empty)? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap] — PASS: edge case line 106 "MUST NOT include that parameter in the upstream request."
- [x] CHK026 Are network-unreachable and timeout scenarios required to surface a clear error AND honor the cancellation signal? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-011, §SC-009] — PASS: edge case line 107 + FR-011 + SC-009.
- [x] CHK027 Is the batch-probabilities over-limit (>100 IDs) behavior required to be documented in the tool description, with stated behavior following the upstream API? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-014] — PASS: edge case line 111 "MUST document the limit in its description; behavior beyond the limit follows the upstream API."
- [x] CHK028 Are requirements defined for the no-config-at-all plugin load case (read-only tools still work; auth tools return key-required error)? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-006] — PASS: edge case line 112 + FR-006 (reconciled to rely-on-upstream: read-only tools function; auth tools surface upstream 401 as auth-category error).

## Non-Functional Requirements — API Integrity

- [x] CHK029 Is the rate-limit (500 req/min/IP) handling requirement specified as "surface errors clearly, no client-side throttling, host paces calls" — and is the responsibility boundary (integration vs. host) explicit? [Non-Functional, Spec §Assumptions, §Edge Cases, Gap] — PASS (fixed 2026-06-20): FR-022 added elevating the constraint to a binding requirement — no client-side throttling, unbatched inventory, 429 as upstream-category error, explicit responsibility boundary (integration surfaces; host paces).
- [x] CHK030 Is the HTTPS-only transport requirement for API-key transmission stated as a hard non-functional requirement, not merely an assumption? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-007, §Assumptions] — PASS: FR-007 "MUST be transmitted only over HTTPS" — stated as MUST, not assumption.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Is the upstream-API-alpha dependency (behavior may change without notice) documented as an assumption with a stated update obligation? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-004] — PASS: Assumptions document alpha-state + update obligation.
- [x] CHK032 Is the WebSocket-out-of-scope boundary documented so it is not mistaken for a missing requirement? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions] — PASS: Assumptions explicitly state "out of scope... documented future enhancement."
- [x] CHK033 Is the stdio-only transport scope for the MCP server documented as an explicit exclusion (no SSE/HTTP this release)? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions] — PASS: Assumptions explicitly state "single supported transport is stdio; other transports (SSE, HTTP) are out of scope."

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Does "human-readable formatting for LLM consumption" (FR-003) conflict with "MUST NOT mutate response bodies" (FR-003) — and is the exception clause precise enough to resolve the tension? [Conflict, Spec §FR-003] — PASS (resolved Q4): formatting exception removed; FR-003 mandates verbatim passthrough with zero transformation. No conflict remains.
- [x] CHK035 Is "representative read-only calls" (SC-010) defined with a selection criterion, or is it ambiguous which endpoints count as representative? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-010, §FR-020] — PASS (resolved Q2): "representative" replaced with "all ~16 public read-only endpoints (full coverage, no sampling)." No longer ambiguous.

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items reference spec sections via `[Spec §FR-NNN]`, `[Spec §SC-NNN]`, `[Spec §US]`, `[Spec §Edge Cases]`, `[Spec §Assumptions]`; markers `[Gap]`, `[Ambiguity]`, `[Conflict]`, `[Assumption]` flag requirement-quality concerns
- Items test the REQUIREMENTS' quality, not the implementation's behavior
- Author self-check depth: standard (~35 items across 8 categories, focused on API & integration dimension)
